summary: Small PL subsystem matching slice split from a larger matching-improvements branch. The only file change in this slice was in `src/melee/pl/pltrick.c`, where local `Fighter*` declarations inside `pl_80038144` were reordered so `victim_fp` is declared before `attacker_fp`. The PR body frames this as part of a directory-scoped split from #2617 to make matching improvements easier to review; no review comments or detailed rationale were present.

key_files.reason: Only changed file; adjusts local declaration order in `pl_80038144`.

what_changed: In `pl_80038144(HSD_GObj* attacker_gobj, HSD_GObj* victim_gobj, s32 x18d4_int, ft_800898B4_t* ev_data, u16 attack_instance, s32 arg5, s32 source_ply)`, the local declaration order changed from `attacker_fp`, `victim_fp`, `attacker_fp2` to `victim_fp`, `attacker_fp`, `attacker_fp2`.
The PR was split by directory/subsystem from a larger source branch, with this slice limited to `src/melee/pl`.
The body reports the broader unsplit branch had improved matched code/data and no broken matches/regressions, but this specific slice only contains a one-line reorder.
smart_moves: Kept the PR extremely narrow: one file, one local declaration ordering change, making it easy to review and low risk.
Used a subsystem/pathspec split from a larger branch so PL-specific matching changes could be evaluated independently.
Preserved types and symbol names while only adjusting declaration order, which is a common low-impact tactic for influencing stack/register allocation in decomp matching.
decomp_lessons: Local variable declaration order can matter for matching, even when the source-level semantics are unchanged.
For decomp PRs split from large matching branches, directory-scoped slices reduce review burden and make it easier to isolate regressions.
When a change is only a declaration reorder, avoid inferring behavioral intent; treat it as a codegen/matching adjustment unless comments or tests say otherwise.
naming_conventions: `pl_80038144` follows the existing subsystem-address placeholder naming convention for not-yet-fully-named functions.
`attacker_gobj` and `victim_gobj` distinguish `HSD_GObj*` parameters by combat role.
`attacker_fp`, `victim_fp`, and `attacker_fp2` use the common `fp` suffix for `Fighter*` locals.
`acp` and `acp2` appear to be existing short local names for `plActionStats*`; this PR did not rename them.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Reordered local pointer declarations in `pl_80038144`, likely to affect compiler allocation or stack layout for matching.
No assembly excerpt, map delta, or per-function match result was included, so the exact matching effect of the declaration reorder is not evidenced in the slice.
The PR body says the full unsplit branch had green CI and no broken matches/regressions before splitting, but per-slice matching results are not shown in the provided context.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The PR body itself documents the review strategy: split from #2617 by directory/subsystem so matching improvements are easier to review.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs split from #2617 to compare matching tactics across subsystems.
Search for later changes to `pl_80038144` to see whether the placeholder name or local variable names were refined.
Check CI/decomp report for PR #2628 if available to confirm whether this local declaration reorder produced a measurable function match.
